growth_records.strain: ATCC 25755 (wild-type) and 12C6+ irradiation mutant 'Positive' strain (e.g. QSH-M-F75-6-1)

up.method: HPLC; initial glucose 180 g/L (highest of 0-180 g/L series)

sec.method: GC; maximum endpoint titer (range 15.5-56.3 g/L over glucose 60-180 g/L)
GC; endpoint (range 4.8-13.5 g/L over glucose 60-180 g/L)

growth_records.cite: Cao L et al. A Series of Efficient Umbrella Modeling Strategies to Track Irradiation-Mutation Strains Improving Butyric Acid Production From the Pre-development Earlier Stage Point of View. Frontiers in Bioengineering and Biotechnology (2021). DOI:10.3389/fbioe.2021.609345. [PMC8242359]
growth_records.method: model parameter (maximal specific growth-rate mu_m=0.48/h fitted in Monod model)
growth_records.snippet: Butyric acid production ranged from 15.5 to 56.3 g/L and acetic acid production from 4.8 to 13.5 g/L for original glucose concentrations from 60 ... 150, and 180 g/L... no growth could be found when the biomass concentration exceeded 16 g/L
growth_records.notes: Prokaryote (Clostridium) anaerobic batch fermentation; strain irradiation-mutant. Products are experimental GC endpoint titers (g/L): butyric max 56.3, acetic 13.5 at high glucose (180 g/L). mu_m=0.48/h is a fitted Monod model max (not directly measured); td=ln2/0.48=1.44 h. Yield-factors (butyric 0.973, acetic 0.997, biomass 0.812 g/g) are stoichiometric model parameters. OD-DCW conversion 0.412±0.012 g DCW/L per OD600 unit. Rates given as g/L not gDW/h (no per-biomass specific rates or time-course in body text).

up.method: HPLC; lowest non-zero glucose of 60-180 g/L series

sec.method: GC; low end of butyric range (15.5-56.3 g/L)
GC; low end of acetic range (4.8-13.5 g/L)

yields.note: see paired record; yields are model stoichiometric factors

growth_records.cite: Cao L et al. A Series of Efficient Umbrella Modeling Strategies to Track Irradiation-Mutation Strains Improving Butyric Acid Production From the Pre-development Earlier Stage Point of View. Frontiers in Bioengineering and Biotechnology (2021). DOI:10.3389/fbioe.2021.609345. [PMC8242359]
growth_records.method: not separately reported at this glucose level
growth_records.snippet: Butyric acid production ranged from 15.5 to 56.3 g/L and acetic acid production from 4.8 to 13.5 g/L for original glucose concentrations from 60 ... 180 g/L
growth_records.notes: Low-glucose (60 g/L) endpoint of the glucose-titration batch series. Pairing of 60 g/L glucose with the LOW ends of the product ranges (butyric 15.5, acetic 4.8 g/L) is inferred from the text phrasing 'ranged from X to Y for glucose from 60...180 g/L' (per-point values are only in Figure 4, not numerically in body text). Anaerobic Clostridium batch. No specific gDW/h rates reported.

growth_records.medium: Clostridial growth medium (CGM), 60 g/L glucose (P2 rich-medium composition stated in Methods)
growth_records.cite: Zhou et al. Radiation induces acid tolerance of Clostridium tyrobutyricum and enhances bioproduction of butyric acid through a metabolic switch. Biotechnology for Biofuels (2014). DOI:10.1186/1754-6834-7-22. [PMC3931924]
growth_records.snippet: the butyric acid yield from glucose increased significantly, from 0.43 g.g-1 for the wild-type strains ... acetic acid yield from glucose ... approximately 0.11 g.g-1 for the wild-type strain ... butyrate/acetate ratio (g/g) increased from
growth_records.notes: Wild-type C. tyrobutyricum ATCC 25755, batch anaerobic fermentation, CGM with 60 g/L glucose, 37C, pH 5.5-6.0 (record uses 6.0). mu=0.42 h-1 reported for wild type (text: 'wild type (mu=0.38 to 0.42 h-1)'; abstract: 0.42). Doubling time computed td=ln2/mu=0.6931/0.42=1.65 h (at mu=0.38 td=1.82 h). mu calculated by authors from semi-log slope of biomass dry weight (BDW) vs time (ln transformation, y=mx+b). Butyrate final titer 22.7 g/L (abstract). Butyrate yield 0.43 g/g glucose; acetate yield 0.11 g/g; butyrate/acetate 3.99 (Fig 5). Glucose 60 g/L completely consumed but qGlucose in mmol/gDW/h not derivable (biomass yield Yxs not reported). CO2 and H2 also produced but not quantified. Full P2 rich-medium composition listed from Methods; production runs (Fig 4/5) state CGM with 60 g/L glucose - CGM full composition not separately itemized, so P2 composition is given as the documented rich medium.

sec.method: measured, stated similar to wild type (Fig 5)

growth_records.cite: Zhou et al. Radiation induces acid tolerance of Clostridium tyrobutyricum and enhances bioproduction of butyric acid through a metabolic switch. Biotechnology for Biofuels (2014). DOI:10.1186/1754-6834-7-22. [PMC3931924]
growth_records.snippet: the butyric acid yield from glucose increased ... to 0.56 g.g-1 for the strain irradiated at 68 AMeV and a dose of 30 Gy ... The acetic acid produced by the strain irradiated at 68 AMeV and doses of 30 and 40 Gy were similar to that from th
growth_records.notes: Irradiated strain 68 AMeV/30 Gy. Butyrate yield 0.56 g/g glucose (Fig 5A). mu=0.42+/-0.03 h-1 (text: '20 and 30 Gy irradiated strains at 68 AMeV had ... almost identical specific growth rate of mu=0.42+/-0.03 h-1'); td=ln2/0.42=1.65 h. Acetic acid yield stated similar to wild type (~0.11 g/g). Butyrate/control titer ratio=1.31 (text); derived final butyrate titer = 22.7 g/L (WT) x 1.31 = 29.7 g/L (flagged is_experimental:false, derived). Glucose 60 g/L completely consumed. qGlucose not derivable (Yxs not reported).

sec.method: measured, stated similar to wild type

growth_records.cite: Zhou et al. Radiation induces acid tolerance of Clostridium tyrobutyricum and enhances bioproduction of butyric acid through a metabolic switch. Biotechnology for Biofuels (2014). DOI:10.1186/1754-6834-7-22. [PMC3931924]
growth_records.snippet: 0.59 g.g-1 for the strain irradiated at 68 AMeV and a dose of 40 Gy ... The AK activity was reduced by approximately ... 26% for the strains irradiated at 68 AMeV and 40 Gy.
growth_records.notes: Irradiated strain 68 AMeV/40 Gy. Butyrate yield 0.59 g/g glucose (Fig 5A). Specific growth rate not explicitly reported for this dose (irradiated-strain overall range mu=0.38 to 0.21 h-1); left null. Acetate yield stated similar to wild type (~0.11 g/g). AK (acetate kinase) activity reduced 26% vs WT. Butyrate/control titer ratio=1.41; derived final butyrate = 22.7 x 1.41 = 32.0 g/L (is_experimental:false, derived). Glucose 60 g/L completely consumed; qGlucose not derivable (Yxs not reported).

growth_records.cite: Zhou et al. Radiation induces acid tolerance of Clostridium tyrobutyricum and enhances bioproduction of butyric acid through a metabolic switch. Biotechnology for Biofuels (2014). DOI:10.1186/1754-6834-7-22. [PMC3931924]
growth_records.snippet: 0.63 g.g-1 for the strain irradiated at 114 AMeV and a dose of 30 Gy ... acetic acid yield from glucose ... around 0.08 g.g-1 for the strain irradiated at 114 AMeV and 30 Gy ... mu=0.26+/-0.03 h-1 (30 Gy)
growth_records.notes: Irradiated strain 114 AMeV/30 Gy. mu=0.26+/-0.03 h-1 (text, calculated from BDW semi-log slope); td=ln2/0.26=2.67 h. Butyrate yield 0.63 g/g glucose (Fig 5B); acetate yield 0.08 g/g. AK activity reduced 31% vs WT. Butyrate/control titer ratio=1.37; derived final butyrate = 22.7 x 1.37 = 31.1 g/L (is_experimental:false, derived). Glucose 60 g/L completely consumed; qGlucose not derivable (Yxs not reported).

growth_records.strain: ATCC 25755 irradiated 114 AMeV, 40 Gy (12C6+ heavy-ion; best butyrate producer)
growth_records.cite: Zhou et al. Radiation induces acid tolerance of Clostridium tyrobutyricum and enhances bioproduction of butyric acid through a metabolic switch. Biotechnology for Biofuels (2014). DOI:10.1186/1754-6834-7-22. [PMC3931924]
growth_records.snippet: specific growth rate decreased by 50% (from 0.42 to 0.21 h-1) and the final concentration of butyrate increased by 68% (from 22.7 to 33.4 g.L-1) for the strain irradiated at 114 AMeV and 40 Gy ... 0.66 g.g-1 ... acetic acid yield ... around
growth_records.notes: Best butyrate producer: 114 AMeV/40 Gy. mu=0.21+/-0.02 h-1 (abstract + text; 50% lower than WT 0.42; calculated from BDW semi-log slope, regression y=0.2129x-2.6457, R2=0.9765). td=ln2/0.21=3.30 h. Final butyrate titer 33.4 g/L (abstract, +68% vs WT 22.7). NOTE: in-text 'produced more butyrate (60 g.L-1)' appears inconsistent with abstract 33.4 g/L and 'over 32 g/L'; treated as likely typo, used 33.4 g/L. Butyrate yield 0.66 g/g glucose (could exceed 0.66 if lag-phase glucose neglected per authors); acetate yield 0.07 g/g; butyrate/acetate 5.82 g/g. AK activity -47%, PTA +129%, PTB similar vs WT (impaired PTA-AK pathway shifts flux to butyrate). Glucose 60 g/L completely consumed; qGlucose not derivable (Yxs not reported). CO2 + H2 co-products (not quantified).

growth_records.strain: mutant No. FS-ZKJ-D80-85-8 (secondary 12C6+ heavy-ion irradiation mutant)
growth_records.medium: chemically defined P2 medium (in serum bottles), 50 g/L glucose + 10.0 g/L butyric acid

up.method: batch fermentation; glucose consumption time-course shown in Fig 5 (figure only, no numeric rate in text)

sec.method: main fermentation product; time-course in Fig 5 (figure only)
GAS production time-course in Fig 5 (figure only)

product_yields.note: butyrate:acetate (B/A) ratio used as a quality metric but no numeric mol/mol yield given in text

growth_records.cite: Zhou X et al. The acquisition of Clostridium tyrobutyricum mutants with improved bioproduction under acidic conditions after two rounds of heavy-ion beam irradiation. Scientific Reports (2016). DOI:10.1038/srep29968. [PMC4947956]
growth_records.snippet: Individual batch cultures were conducted in chemically defined P2-medium (in serum bottles) containing 50 g/L glucose and supplemented with 10.0 g/L butyric acid. Using xm = 1.543 g/L dry weight and x = 0.830 g/L dry weight from our experim
growth_records.notes: Batch butyrate-inhibition assay for secondary irradiation mutant FS-ZKJ-D80-85-8. Growth rate is model-derived from experimental biomass (μm = slope of eq(10) = 0.1934 1/h). td computed = ln2/0.1934 = 3.58 h. Temperature 37 C confirmed in text (Fig 5 conditions). pH not specified for this specific 10 g/L-butyrate curve (medium showed biphasic response to two pH values). No numeric substrate-uptake or product-secretion rates in the text; Fig 5 shows time-courses of butyric acid, acetic acid, gas, OD600 and glucose consumption but values are figure-only. P2 medium full composition not tabulated (chemically defined Reinforced Clostridial SEM-defined P2).

growth_records.strain: mutant No. FS-ZKJ-D80-85-8 (secondary 12C6+ heavy-ion irradiation mutant)
growth_records.medium: chemically defined P2 medium (in serum bottles), 50 g/L glucose + 12.0 g/L butyric acid

up.method: batch fermentation; consumption in Fig 5 (figure only)

growth_records.cite: Zhou X et al. The acquisition of Clostridium tyrobutyricum mutants with improved bioproduction under acidic conditions after two rounds of heavy-ion beam irradiation. Scientific Reports (2016). DOI:10.1038/srep29968. [PMC4947956]
growth_records.snippet: Individual batch cultures were conducted in chemically defined P2-medium (in serum bottles) containing 50 g/L glucose and supplemented with 12.0 g/L butyric acid. Taking xm = 1.386 g/L dry weight and x = 0.612 g/L dry weight from our experi
growth_records.notes: Same mutant, higher butyrate stress (12 g/L). Growth reduced vs 10 g/L condition (μm 0.1434 vs 0.1934 1/h), demonstrating dose-dependent butyrate inhibition; this mutant still grew notably above 12 g/L butyric acid. td computed = ln2/0.1434 = 4.83 h. 37 C; pH not given for this specific curve. No numeric uptake/secretion rates in text.

growth_records.medium: Modified CGM (Clostridial Growth Medium), no formate
growth_records.cite: Ma, Chao et al. Rebalancing Redox to Improve Biobutanol Production by Clostridium tyrobutyricum. Bioengineering (Basel) (2015). DOI:10.3390/bioengineering3010002. [PMC5597160]
growth_records.snippet: the wild-type produced 9.02 g/L of butyrate and no butanol, as expected (serum-bottle baseline, modified CGM without formate)
growth_records.notes: Serum-bottle baseline (Figure 4A). WT native fermentation: butyrate 9.02 g/L, butanol 0. Titers only (g/L), no rate/μ reported for this baseline. Anaerobic acidogen naturally producing butyrate, acetate, CO2, H2.

up.method: consumed (used for yield 0.14 g/g) but qS not tabulated

growth_records.cite: Ma, Chao et al. Rebalancing Redox to Improve Biobutanol Production by Clostridium tyrobutyricum. Bioengineering (Basel) (2015). DOI:10.3390/bioengineering3010002. [PMC5597160]
growth_records.snippet: The CTC-adhE2 produced 8.78 g/L of butyrate and 2.51 g/L of butanol ... Table 4 (this study): Bottle Glucose butanol titer 2.6 g/L, yield 0.14 g/g, productivity 0.03 g/L/h
growth_records.notes: Serum-bottle baseline (Figure 4B / Table 4 'this study'). Butanol 2.51 g/L (text) ~ 2.6 g/L (Table 4). Butyrate 8.78 g/L. Yield 0.14 g butanol/g glucose, volumetric productivity 0.03 g/L/h. Titers, not specific rates; no μ tabulated for bottle.

up.units: g gDW-1 h-1 (=9.99 mmol gDW-1 h-1)

growth_records.cite: Ma, Chao et al. Rebalancing Redox to Improve Biobutanol Production by Clostridium tyrobutyricum. Bioengineering (Basel) (2015). DOI:10.3390/bioengineering3010002. [PMC5597160]
growth_records.snippet: Table 3: CTC-adhE2 SF: Cell growth 0.18 ± 0.01 h-1, Biomass yield 0.10 g/g, Butanol 3.18 g/L (yield 0.05, prod 0.12), Butyrate 13.22 (0.27,0.33), Acetate 4.15 (0.06,0.14), Ethanol 0.11 (0.001,0.006)
growth_records.notes: Table 3 (2-L fed-batch, 37C, pH6.0, 100rpm). μ=0.18/h (reported); td=ln2/0.18=3.85 h. Derived qGlucose=μ/Yxs=0.18/0.10=1.80 g gDW-1 h-1; /180.16*1000=9.99 mmol gDW-1 h-1. Secretion rates are reported volumetric productivities (g/L/h); yields g/g glucose consumed.

growth_records.medium: CGM + sodium formate + vitamin B12 + methyl viologen hydrate

up.units: g gDW-1 h-1 (=9.87 mmol gDW-1 h-1)

growth_records.cite: Ma, Chao et al. Rebalancing Redox to Improve Biobutanol Production by Clostridium tyrobutyricum. Bioengineering (Basel) (2015). DOI:10.3390/bioengineering3010002. [PMC5597160]
growth_records.snippet: Table 3: CTC-adhE2 SF,B12,MVH: growth 0.16 h-1, biomass yield 0.09, Butanol 6.14 g/L (0.11,0.14), Butyrate 9.32 (0.20,0.26), Acetate 1.42 (0.02,0.07), Ethanol 0.25 (0.003,0.008)
growth_records.notes: Table 3 process-engineered control. μ=0.16/h; td=0.693/0.16=4.33 h. qGlucose=0.16/0.09=1.78 g gDW-1 h-1=9.87 mmol gDW-1 h-1. Productivities reported (g/L/h). SF 1 g/L, B12 0.001 g/L, MVH 0.1 g/L per Section 4.2.

up.units: g gDW-1 h-1 (=9.25 mmol gDW-1 h-1)

growth_records.cite: Ma, Chao et al. Rebalancing Redox to Improve Biobutanol Production by Clostridium tyrobutyricum. Bioengineering (Basel) (2015). DOI:10.3390/bioengineering3010002. [PMC5597160]
growth_records.snippet: Table 3: CTC-fdh-adhE2 SF: growth 0.15 h-1, biomass yield 0.09, Butanol 8.83 g/L (0.16,0.17), Butyrate 7.72 (0.19,0.22), Acetate 0.69 (0.02,0.05), Ethanol 0.24 (0.004,0.010)
growth_records.notes: Table 3 redox-engineered. μ=0.15/h; td=0.693/0.15=4.62 h. qGlucose=0.15/0.09=1.667 g gDW-1 h-1=9.25 mmol gDW-1 h-1. Productivities reported (g/L/h).

growth_records.medium: CGM + sodium formate + vitamin B12 + methyl viologen hydrate

up.units: g gDW-1 h-1 (=10.41 mmol gDW-1 h-1)

growth_records.cite: Ma, Chao et al. Rebalancing Redox to Improve Biobutanol Production by Clostridium tyrobutyricum. Bioengineering (Basel) (2015). DOI:10.3390/bioengineering3010002. [PMC5597160]
growth_records.snippet: Table 3: CTC-fdh-adhE2 SF,B12,MVH: growth 0.15 h-1, biomass yield 0.08, Butanol 12.34 g/L (0.23,0.26), Butyrate 5.05 (0.10,0.15), Acetate 0.26 (0.003,0.01), Ethanol 0.28 (0.004,0.011)
growth_records.notes: Best condition (integrated cell+process engineering): butanol 12.34 g/L, 3.88-fold over CTC-adhE2 SF. μ=0.15/h; td=0.693/0.15=4.62 h. qGlucose=μ/Yxs=0.15/0.08=1.875 g gDW-1 h-1=10.41 mmol gDW-1 h-1. Butanol selectivity 0.69 g/g total products. Productivities reported (g/L/h).
